name: CRUD/conditional-delete
description: Conditional delete (DELETE /Patient?search-params) conformance tests: no search criteria is rejected, no matching resources succeeds idempotently, exactly one matching resource is removed, and multiple matching resources are rejected with 412 unless the server opts into a bulk conditional delete extension. Uses Patient fixtures tagged with unique identifiers (http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-delete) to avoid collisions with other suites.

variable.name: oneMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient unconditionally created in setup that carries identifier CD-ONEMATCH

operation.description: Unconditionally create the Patient that the single-match delete test will remove (CD-ONEMATCH)
Unconditionally create the first of two Patients sharing identifier CD-MULTI
Unconditionally create the second of two Patients sharing identifier CD-MULTI

test.name: Conditional delete with no search criteria is rejected
test.description: DELETE /Patient with no query string at all. A conditional delete requires selective search criteria; with none, the server must reject the request. 412 Precondition Failed is the primary expectation (matches the 'not selective enough' validation used by conditional create/update/patch); 400 Bad Request is accepted as a warningOnly alternative since the FHIR spec does not mandate a specific status for this case.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient with no query string

assert.description: Alternative: 400 Bad Request is also accepted

test.name: Conditional delete with no matching resources succeeds
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-delete|CD-NOMATCH. No resource carries that identifier, so the delete is a no-op and the server must still respond with a 2xx success status (idempotent delete semantics).

assert.description: Must return a 2xx success status (204 No Content is typical)

test.name: Conditional delete with exactly one matching resource removes it
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-delete|CD-ONEMATCH. Exactly one Patient carries that identifier (created in setup), so the server must delete it and return a 2xx success status. A subsequent read of the deleted resource must return 410 Gone (or 404 Not Found as a warningOnly alternative).

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-ONEMATCH, matches the single seeded Patient
Attempt to read the deleted Patient

assert.description: Alternative: 404 Not Found is also accepted

test.name: Conditional delete with multiple matching resources fails
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-delete|CD-MULTI. Two Patients already carry that identifier (created in setup), so the criteria is not selective enough to identify a single resource; without an explicit multi-delete opt-in the server must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-MULTI, matches both seeded Patients, no multi-delete opt-in

test.name: Conditional delete can remove multiple matches when the server opts in via _count
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-delete|CD-MULTI&_count=100. Some FHIR servers (e.g. Microsoft FHIR Server) support an explicit opt-in, via a _count parameter greater than 1, to delete every matching resource in one request. This is a server extension, not mandated by the base FHIR R4 spec, so the whole test is warningOnly to keep the suite portable across servers that reject multi-match conditional deletes outright.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-MULTI&_count=100, opting into bulk delete of both matches

assert.description: Server-extension: expect a 2xx success status when bulk conditional delete is supported

operation.description: Teardown: conditional delete the CD-NOMATCH Patient (should already be absent)
Teardown: conditional delete the CD-ONEMATCH Patient (should already be deleted by the tests)
Teardown: conditional delete any remaining CD-MULTI Patients (best effort, in case the server does not support bulk conditional delete)
